content.text:
You are resolving a real issue in the facebook/zstd repository (c).

## Issue
Cap hashlog for row based matchfinder, chainlog for short cache matchfinders

[This assert](https://github.com/embg/zstd/blob/dev/lib/compress/zstd_compress_internal.h#L785) which was added as part of short cache has uncovered two bugs:
* The short cache PR only validates that hashLog is <= 24 bits. We need to do the same for chainLog, this has showed up in some CI failures.
* The row based matchfinder needs to have hashLog capped at 28 bits (24 + 4). The assert linked above can currently be triggered by `./zstd -o /dev/null -7 < ~/silesia.tar --zstd=hlog=29`

These bugs can't lead to data corruption, but they do have some bad effects:
* Can regress compression ratio by corrupting the hashtable
* Every time the assert fails, that is undefined behavior (shift by larger than width of type)
* Cause CI failures for debug builds

Code pointer: https://github.com/facebook/zstd/blob/4f7183d887789d4d2bb2e5af850c427f1df725ff/lib/compress/zstd_compress.c#L1443

## Task
Investigate the codebase and make the minimal source changes needed to resolve the issue.
Do NOT modify, add, or delete any test files — the grader applies its own tests.
Edit the code in place. When you are confident the fix is complete, stop.

arguments.task: Investigate zstd compression parameter validation/capping for hashLog and chainLog, especially short cache matchfinders and row based matchfinder. Find likely edit sites around zstd_compress.c pointer, related constants/assert in zstd_compress_internal.h, and existing tests/verification commands if any. Return minimal relevant snippets and explain control flow.

arguments.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
terms=['ZSTD_CDictIndicesAreTagged','ZSTD_row','ROW_HASH','SHORT_CACHE','ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS','hashLog']
for p in ['lib/compress/zstd_compress.c','lib/compress/zstd_compress_internal.h','lib/compress/zstd_fast.c','lib/compress/zstd_double_fast.c']:
    lines=Path(p).read_text().splitlines()
    printed=set()
    for i,l in enumerate(lines):
        if any(t in l for t in terms):
            start=max(1,i+1-8); end=min(len(lines),i+1+12)
            key=(start,end)
            if key in printed: continue
            printed.add(key)
            print(f'--- {p}:{start}-{end}')
            for j in range(start-1,end): print(f'{j+1}: {lines[j]}')
PY

content.thinking:
**Considering caching strategies**

I'm thinking about how to manage cache limits effectively. It seems I need to ensure that the chains are short when using dfast. If I look at chainLog, adding 8 may exceed a cap of 32. lib/zstd.h 356                               * Must be clamped between ZSTD_HASHLOG_MIN and ZSTD_HASHLOG_MAX.
lib/zstd.h 362                               * Must be clamped between ZSTD_CHAINLOG_MIN and ZSTD_CHAINLOG_MAX.
lib/zstd.h 404                               * Must be clamped between ZSTD_HASHLOG_MIN and ZSTD_HASHLOG_MAX
lib/zstd.h 1191 #define ZSTD_HASHLOG_MAX       ((ZSTD_WINDOWLOG_MAX < 30) ? ZSTD_WINDOWLOG_MAX : 30)
lib/zstd.h 1193 #define ZSTD_CHAINLOG_MAX_32     29
lib/zstd.h 1194 #define ZSTD_CHAINLOG_MAX_64     30
lib/zstd.h 1195 #define ZSTD_CHAINLOG_MAX      ((int)(sizeof(size_t) == 4 ? ZSTD_CHAINLOG_MAX_32 : ZSTD_CHAINLOG_MAX_64))
lib/zstd.h 1220 #define ZSTD_LDM_HASHLOG_MAX      ZSTD_HASHLOG_MAX

11: /*-*************************************
12: *  Dependencies
13: ***************************************/
14: #include "../common/zstd_deps.h"  /* INT_MAX, ZSTD_memset, ZSTD_memcpy */
15: #include "../common/mem.h"
16: #include "hist.h"           /* HIST_countFast_wksp */
17: #define FSE_STATIC_LINKING_ONLY   /* FSE_encodeSymbol */
18: #include "../common/fse.h"
19: #define HUF_STATIC_LINKING_ONLY
20: #include "../common/huf.h"
21: #include "zstd_compress_internal.h"
22: #include "zstd_compress_sequences.h"
23: #include "zstd_compress_literals.h"
24: #include "zstd_fast.h"
25: #include "zstd_double_fast.h"
26: #include "zstd_lazy.h"
27: #include "zstd_opt.h"
28: #include "zstd_ldm.h"
29: #include "zstd_compress_superblock.h"
30: #include  "../common/bits.h"      /* ZSTD_highbit32 */
31: 
32: /* ***************************************************************
33: *  Tuning parameters
34: *****************************************************************/
35: /*!
36:  * COMPRESS_HEAPMODE :
37:  * Select how default decompression function ZSTD_compress() allocates its context,
38:  * on stack (0, default), or into heap (1).
39:  * Note that functions with explicit context such as ZSTD_compressCCtx() are unaffected.
40:  */
41: #ifndef ZSTD_COMPRESS_HEAPMODE
42: #  define ZSTD_COMPRESS_HEAPMODE 0
43: #endif
44: 
45: /*!
46:  * ZSTD_HASHLOG3_MAX :
47:  * Maximum size of the hash table dedicated to find 3-bytes matches,
48:  * in log format, aka 17 => 1 << 17 == 128Ki positions.
49:  * This structure is only used in zstd_opt.
50:  * Since allocation is centralized for all strategies, it has to be known here.
51:  * The actual (selected) size of the hash table is then stored in ZSTD_matchState_t.hashLog3,
52:  * so that zstd_opt.c doesn't need to know about this constant.
53:  */
54: #ifndef ZSTD_HASHLOG3_MAX
55: #  define ZSTD_HASHLOG3_MAX 17
56: #endif
57: 
58: /*-*************************************
59: *  Helper functions
60: ***************************************/
61: /* ZSTD_compressBound()
62:  * Note that the result from this function is only valid for
63:  * the one-pass compression functions.
64:  * When employing the streaming mode,
65:  * if flushes are frequently altering the size of blocks,
66:  * the overhead from block headers can make the compressed data larger
67:  * than the return value of ZSTD_compressBound().
68:  */
69: size_t ZSTD_compressBound(size_t srcSize) {
70:     size_t const r = ZSTD_COMPRESSBOUND(srcSize);
71:     if (r==0) return ERROR(srcSize_wrong);
72:     return r;
73: }
74: 
75: 
76: /*-*************************************
77: *  Context memory management
78: ***************************************/
79: struct ZSTD_CDict_s {

I wonder if I can’t use it before the macro? It seems that a modification will be necessary, so I'll need to plan for that edit. Let’s ensure everything is in order for proper functionality!
